findings.fix: Remove `@[reducible]`; add the missing simp/characteristic lemmas needed by users rather than exposing the instance body.
findings.issue: `Comodule.Corestrict` is marked `@[reducible]`, making the full structure body part of the public reduction surface instead of relying on API lemmas.

findings.evidence: Lines 50-51 define `corestrictCoact`; lines 131-135 only prove `coact = corestrictCoact`, with no `rg` hit for `corestrictCoact_apply` or `corestrict_coact_apply`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` lemmas such as `corestrictCoact_apply` and/or a direct `corestrict_coact_apply` giving the evaluated coaction formula.
findings.issue: The main coaction lemma rewrites only to the public helper `corestrictCoact`, whose actual `(TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id f.toLinearMap) (coact m)` formula has no simp/apply theorem, so users must unfold the helper to use the construction.

findings.evidence: `rg` shows only `corestrictCoact_id` at lines 139-143, while Mathlib provides `CoalgHom.comp` and simp lemmas at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/Hom.lean:215-235`.
findings.fix: Add `[simp]` composition lemmas for `corestrictCoact`, `corestrictHom`, and preferably the bundled functor, e.g. corestricting along `g.comp f` agrees with corestricting along `f` then `g`.
findings.issue: Corestriction is not characterized under composition of coalgebra morphisms; only identity is exposed at the raw coaction-map level, so the promised functoriality in the coalgebra map is unusable without unfolding.

summary: The corestriction surface is too thin for downstream use without unfolding: it exposes the construction body and lacks the characteristic simp lemmas for the actual coaction formula and composition in the coalgebra map.
